windows.transcript: look at the flame and think their candle wasn't working properly. They expected a tall, lively flame like a cotton wick. Instead, they're getting a low flickering
windows.embed_text_v2: finished wood-wick candle burning with a low flickering flame in a clear glass tumbler, charred wooden wick and amber melt pool, blurred green outdoor background
windows.summary_v2: Close-up of a finished wood-wick candle burning with a low flickering flame in a clear glass jar outdoors.

seconds_v2.desc: Extreme close-up of a finished candle in a clear glass tumbler with a charred wooden wick at center; the wick is barely lit, set against a blurred sunlit green outdoor background.
The wooden wick catches a small yellow flame on one corner; a shallow melt pool of amber wax surrounds it inside the glass.
A low single flame burns at the edge of the wood wick, flickering modestly above the pale wax surface.
The flame on the wooden wick grows slightly taller, illuminating the melted wax pool in the glass jar.
Flame dips low again to a small ember-like glow along the crackling wood wick, demonstrating its short flickering burn.
The wood wick shows just a faint flame; the candle continues to burn slowly in the glass against the green bokeh backdrop.
A small teardrop flame returns on the wooden wick, the surrounding wax glossy and partially melted.
Close-up of the wood-wick candle burning with a low flame, melt pool widening in the clear glass tumbler outdoors.
